test_description: This test case evaluates the RAG system's robustness in retrieving a specific, rare, or hard-to-find fact (the 'needle') from a large context ('haystack').
question: [Judge] A high Pixel-zu-Pixel Überlapp (pixel-to-pixel overlap) of at least 95% must be present for at least 50% of the Interferenzpixel to achieve deep surface structures in the structured area of the substrate. (True/False)

gold_context_sources.evidence: Um derartige Strukturtiefen zu erreichen, liegt vorzugsweise ein groRer Pixel-zu-Pixel Uberlapp der benachbarten Interferenzpixel von vorzugsweise wenigstens 95 % fur wenigstens 50 % der Interferenzpixel vor.

ground_truth_answer: The correct answer is True. Reasoning: According to the section titled 'Global-Struktur, insbesondere Global-Punktstruktur' from document LU103057B_LU103057B1, to achieve deep structure depths, a large pixel-to-pixel overlap of at least 95% is preferably present for at least 50% of the interference pixels. This indicates that the statement is true under the specific condition that the overlap is at least 95% for at least half of the pixels, which is explicitly stated in the same document.
